idea-plugin.name: Agent Workbench
idea-plugin.description:
Run and manage AI coding agents — Codex, Claude, Junie, OpenCode, and more — directly inside your IDE.
  • Quick Launch — press Ctrl Ctrl from anywhere to start a new task or continue an existing one. Your current selection, file, and symbol are attached automatically.
  • Launch Profiles — bundle provider, model, reasoning effort, and launch mode into reusable presets (Standard, Full Auto, Brave Mode, or your own) and pick a cheaper, faster, or more careful setup per task without changing your default.
  • AI Review — hold Alt/Option and press Ctrl twice to review local changes or commits with AI and catch bugs, security issues, and style problems before they reach the main branch.
  • All Sessions in One Place — browse, reopen, and resume AI sessions across providers, grouped by project.
  • Terminal Sessions — open regular terminal shells from the same launcher and track them alongside your agent threads; reopen, rename, and archive them just like agent sessions.
  • Persistent Chat Tabs — each session opens in its own editor tab and survives IDE restarts.
  • Separate Window Mode — run AI chat in a dedicated frame so you can work on code and manage tasks side by side.
  • Automatic Context — editor selections, VCS changes, project-view selections, and test results are collected and sent with your prompt so the agent has the context it needs.
  • Context from UI — click any IDE component (tool window, editor, panel) to screenshot it and attach the image as visual context for the agent.
  • Context from Screen — drag to select any screen area and capture it as a screenshot to share with the agent, even outside the IDE.

registryKey.description: Use monochrome line icons on main toolbar buttons, action buttons, and status indicators in Agent Workbench
Open Agent Workbench New Thread actions as an inline prompt before starting the agent session
